description_long: This is a rectangular, three-story apartment building with a gray brick exterior and a hipped roof covered in gray shingles. The facade features a symmetrical arrangement of large rectangular windows, some with white balcony railings, and a central main entrance with a dark awning. The side elevations have fewer windows, with some narrow vertical ones, and the rear side includes additional balconies and a similar window pattern. The building's overall design is simple and functional, with clean lines and minimal ornamentation. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from masonry and concrete, with glass windows and metal balcony railings. The roof is slightly pitched, and the building is likely to contain multiple residential units accessed via the main entrance.
description: A three-story gray brick apartment building with a hipped roof, large windows, and several balconies. The design is symmetrical and functional, with a central entrance and minimal decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, symmetrical window arrangement, and two balconies on the right side.
description_view_1: Side view with a narrow profile, showing fewer windows and no visible balconies.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying more windows and balconies, similar in style to the front but without a main entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with vertical windows and a plain brick wall, lacking balconies.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a regular, symmetrical grid pattern across each floor, with some grouped in pairs and others spaced evenly apart. Balconies are integrated into the window layout, primarily on the front and rear facades.
